assessments.status_rationale: Today’s coverage says the White House “vows to pay T.S.A. workers” during a partial shutdown and DHS says TSA officers will start receiving paychecks soon, which is directionally consistent. However, the prediction requires a publicly posted formal executive action (e.g., EO/presidential memorandum/DHS-OMB directive) explicitly referencing TSA pay; the provided bullets do not confirm that such a specific, public directive has been issued.

Reporting indicates the DHS shutdown/partial shutdown continues and a House GOP funding proposal was spiked, implying no enacted (passed-and-signed) DHS appropriations measure or CR restoring funding yet.

The news discusses the Iran war and U.S. domestic political reactions, but nothing in the bullets indicates the President publicly extended a pause on strikes against Iranian energy facilities beyond 2026-04-06.
No item in today’s bullets mentions a DoD notice of appeal or request for an emergency stay related to an Anthropic-related preliminary injunction.
No referenced news indicates FHFA has published formal written guidance on underwriting/eligibility treatment of crypto-related collateral or crypto-backed mortgage structures.
No referenced news indicates Freddie Mac has publicly confirmed acceptance of any crypto-backed mortgage structure or pilot.
No referenced news indicates any U.S. city/county enacted a moratorium on new large data center construction/permits citing grid/power, water, or environmental strain.
No referenced news indicates DOE published an RFI, study, or strategy document explicitly focused on data center electricity demand growth and grid impacts.
No referenced news indicates FDA issued a public communication explicitly warning about deepfake/manipulated medical images in clinical workflows or fraud.
No referenced news indicates New York State reported year-to-date PIT collections at least $2.0B above forecast and attributed in part to Wall Street/finance income.
